generatedSentence: On a remote, dry island in a forgotten world, an old lumberjack spent his afternoon contemplating his meager investment. He'd often recheck his collection of antique english eggcups, their radiance a small comfort, though often occluded by clouds. He wished for liberties beyond his solitude, hopefully a felicitous future. His hands worked the gnarled leaves, watching the puffy orange clouds and his pet hamster, convinced a mythic purpose was important to define for all who might one day inhabit this place. But this tranquility was shattered when a radioactive tanker truck, leaking dangerous kerogen, delivered an ultimatum to the island. An antivax cult, led by a notorious looter and self-proclaimed impostor, had flocked to the island, claiming it as their own. Their obsession with the toxic substance was illogical, and they threatened to kill anybody who resisted. A desperate islander, armed with a makeshift taser, discovered their passport forgery, but was quickly ko'd by a cultist with a strange feature on his arm – a grotesque jawfish tattoo. It seemed their former life was kaput, replaced by this new, terrifying alternative. The cult's bizarre plan involved overclocking a strange component within the tanker, causing the molecular structure to vibrate violently, threatening to devolve the entire ecosystem into a fiery hail. From their largest printing press, they issued numeric threats, demanding the islanders embrace their ways. A hitman with a knack for chaos joined them, his lust for power driving him to usurp control. The goshawk cried as the barracuda jumps from the water, sensing the impending doom. It was clear the cult intended to ostracise anyone who wouldn't cheer their ostentatious leader, whose tridents of power were an onerous burden. A small band of islanders, including the luminous geckos and a kestrel who soared above, rallied. They had found a secret vaccine, a fortuitous cherry of an idea, to neutralize the toxic tendrils of the kerogen. With seven precise kicks, they disarmed the guards, their arms working in unison. A thwack of a strategically thrown bag of groceries momentarily impacted the cult leader, whose peckish hunger proved his undoing. The wildebeest and elephants were saved, the macaw flew free, and the cult's enemy was defeated. The opalescent bracelet of the island's protector shone as the cultists were captured, their spiders of influence finally broken. A well-aimed boost from the returning wolfs ended the conflict, leaving only the scattered marbles and a few mackerel as a reminder of the strange invasion.
panelSentences: On a remote, dry island in a forgotten world, an old lumberjack spent his afternoon contemplating his meager investment. He'd often recheck his collection of antique english eggcups, their radiance a small comfort, though often occluded by clouds. He wished for liberties beyond his solitude, hopefully a felicitous future. His hands worked the gnarled leaves, watching the puffy orange clouds and his pet hamster, convinced a mythic purpose was important to define for all who might one day inhabit this place.

But this tranquility was shattered when a radioactive tanker truck, leaking dangerous kerogen, delivered an ultimatum to the island. An antivax cult, led by a notorious looter and self-proclaimed impostor, had flocked to the island, claiming it as their own. Their obsession with the toxic substance was illogical, and they threatened to kill anybody who resisted. A desperate islander, armed with a makeshift taser, discovered their passport forgery, but was quickly ko'd by a cultist with a strange feature on his arm – a grotesque jawfish tattoo. It seemed their former life was kaput, replaced by this new, terrifying alternative.

The cult's bizarre plan involved overclocking a strange component within the tanker, causing the molecular structure to vibrate violently, threatening to devolve the entire ecosystem into a fiery hail. From their largest printing press, they issued numeric threats, demanding the islanders embrace their ways. A hitman with a knack for chaos joined them, his lust for power driving him to usurp control. The goshawk cried as the barracuda jumps from the water, sensing the impending doom. It was clear the cult intended to ostracise anyone who wouldn't cheer their ostentatious leader, whose tridents of power were an onerous burden.

A small band of islanders, including the luminous geckos and a kestrel who soared above, rallied. They had found a secret vaccine, a fortuitous cherry of an idea, to neutralize the toxic tendrils of the kerogen. With seven precise kicks, they disarmed the guards, their arms working in unison. A thwack of a strategically thrown bag of groceries momentarily impacted the cult leader, whose peckish hunger proved his undoing. The wildebeest and elephants were saved, the macaw flew free, and the cult's enemy was defeated. The opalescent bracelet of the island's protector shone as the cultists were captured, their spiders of influence finally broken. A well-aimed boost from the returning wolfs ended the conflict, leaving only the scattered marbles and a few mackerel as a reminder of the strange invasion.
rawPanelSentences: On a remote, **dry island** in a forgotten **world**, an old **lumberjack** spent his **afternoon** contemplating his meager **investment**. He'd often **recheck** his collection of antique **english eggcups**, their **radiance** a small comfort, though often **occluded** by clouds. He wished for **liberties** beyond his solitude, **hopefully** a **felicitous** future. His **hands** worked the **gnarled leaves**, watching the **puffy orange** clouds and his pet **hamster**, convinced a **mythic** purpose was **important** to **define** for all who might one day **inhabit** this place.

But this tranquility was shattered when a **radioactive tanker truck**, leaking dangerous **kerogen**, delivered an **ultimatum** to the island. An **antivax** cult, led by a notorious **looter** and self-proclaimed **impostor**, had **flocked** to the island, claiming it as their own. Their **obsession** with the toxic substance was **illogical**, and they threatened to **kill anybody** who resisted. A desperate islander, armed with a makeshift **taser**, discovered their **passport** forgery, but was quickly **ko'd** by a cultist with a strange **feature** on his arm – a grotesque **jawfish** tattoo. It seemed their former life was **kaput**, replaced by this new, terrifying **alternative**.

The cult's bizarre plan involved **overclocking** a strange **component** within the tanker, causing the **molecular** structure to **vibrate** violently, threatening to **devolve** the entire ecosystem into a fiery **hail**. From their **largest** **printing** press, they issued **numeric** threats, demanding the islanders **embrace** their ways. A **hitman** with a **knack** for chaos joined them, his **lust** for power driving him to **usurp** control. The **goshawk** cried as the **barracuda** **jumps** from the water, sensing the impending doom. It was clear the cult intended to **ostracise** anyone who wouldn't **cheer** their **ostentatious** leader, whose **tridents** of power were an **onerous** burden.

A small band of islanders, including the **luminous** **geckos** and a **kestrel** who soared above, rallied. They had found a secret **vaccine**, a **fortuitous** **cherry** of an idea, to neutralize the toxic **tendrils** of the kerogen. With **seven** precise **kicks**, they disarmed the guards, their **arms** working in unison. A **thwack** of a strategically thrown bag of **groceries** momentarily **impacted** the cult leader, whose **peckish** hunger proved his undoing. The **wildebeest** and **elephants** were saved, the **macaw** flew free, and the cult's **enemy** was defeated. The **opalescent bracelet** of the island's protector shone as the cultists were captured, their **spiders** of influence finally broken. A well-aimed **boost** from the returning **wolfs** ended the conflict, leaving only the scattered **marbles** and a few **mackerel** as a reminder of the strange invasion.
imagePrompt: A professional 4-panel comic book page, 2x2 grid, sequential art, vibrant neon futurism, bold ink outlines, cinematic lighting, thick white gutters, clean black borders. A stoic, grizzled lumberjack wearing a patched, leather vest and a wide-brimmed straw hat. TOP-LEFT PANEL: Lumberjack standing on a dry island, looking out at a calm sea, his hands holding an old eggcup, medium shot. TOP-RIGHT PANEL: A futuristic, radioactive tanker truck with tendrils of smoke arriving on the island, cultists with bizarre tattoos emerging, wide shot. BOTTOM-LEFT PANEL: The lumberjack, now with a small group of islanders, using makeshift tools to fight back against the cultists, close-up of a taser being deployed. BOTTOM-RIGHT PANEL: The cult leader, disarmed and defeated, surrounded by luminous geckos and a soaring kestrel, dramatic low-angle shot.

definitions.definition: (with "the") Human collective existence; existence in general.
definitions.example: There will always be lovers, till the world’s end.

definitions.definition: The Universe.
(with "the") The Earth.
definitions.example: People are dying of starvation all over the world.

definitions.definition: A planet, especially one which is inhabited or inhabitable.
definitions.example: Our mission is to travel the galaxy and find new worlds.

definitions.definition: A very large extent of country.
(speculation) A realm, such as planet, containing one or multiple societies of beings, especially intelligent ones.
definitions.example: the world of Narnia; the Wizarding World of Harry Potter; a zombie world

definitions.definition: An individual or group perspective or social setting.
definitions.example: In the world of boxing, good diet is all-important.

definitions.definition: The part of an operating system distributed with the kernel, consisting of the shell and other programs.
A subdivision of a game, consisting of a series of stages or levels that usually share a similar environment or theme.
definitions.example: Have you reached the boss at the end of the ice world?

definitions.definition: The twenty-second trump or major arcana card of the tarot.
A great amount.
definitions.example: Taking a break from work seems to have done her a world of good.

definitions.definition: Age, era
To consider or cause to be considered from a global perspective; to consider as a global whole, rather than making or focussing on national or other distinctions; compare globalise.
To make real; to make worldly.
A person whose work is to fell trees.
A lumberjacket.
To work as a lumberjack, cutting down trees.
The condition of being free from control or restrictions.
definitions.example: The army is here, your liberty is assured.

definitions.definition: The condition of being free from imprisonment, slavery or forced labour.
definitions.example: The prisoners gained their liberty from an underground tunnel.

definitions.definition: The condition of being free to act, believe or express oneself as one chooses.
definitions.example: Everyone has the right to life, liberty and security of person.

definitions.definition: Freedom from excessive government control.
A short period when a sailor is allowed ashore.
definitions.example: We're going on a three-day liberty as soon as we dock.

definitions.definition: (often plural) A breach of social convention.
definitions.example: You needn't take such liberties.

definitions.definition: A local division of government administration in medieval England.
(game of go) an empty space next to a group of stones of the same color.
The act of investing, or state of being invested.
definitions.example: Giving your children a good education is a wise long-term investment.

definitions.definition: A placement of capital in expectation of deriving income or profit from its use or appreciation.
A vestment.
The act of surrounding, blocking up, or besieging by an armed force, or the state of being so surrounded.
A mixture of silica sand and plaster which, by surrounding a wax pattern, creates a negative mold of the form used for casting, among other metals, bronze.
To obstruct, cover, or otherwise block (an opening, a portion of an image, etc.).
To absorb, as a gas by a metal.
Closed or obstructed
(of air) forced upwards by colder, denser air
Spinning or rotary motion given to a ball around the vertical axis, as in billiards or bowling.
definitions.example: You can't hit it directly, but maybe if you give it some english.

definitions.definition: An unusual or unexpected interpretation of a text or idea, a spin, a nuance.
A small dish used to support a boiled egg while it is eaten.
The act of checking again; reverification.
definitions.example: The article needed a complete recheck.

definitions.definition: To check again.
The quality of being radiant, shining, bright or splendid.
The flux of radiation emitted per unit solid angle in a given direction by a unit area of a source.
Any of many marine fish, of the family Opistognathidae, that have large mouths.
Opposing vaccines and vaccination.
Any one out of an indefinite number of persons; anyone; any person.
definitions.example: Anybody will do.

definitions.definition: A person of some consideration or standing.
definitions.example: Everybody who wants to be anybody will come to Jake's party.

definitions.definition: The part of the day from noon or lunchtime until sunset, evening, or suppertime or 6pm.
The later part of anything, often with implications of decline.
A party or social event held in the afternoon.
(more often in the plural) In the afternoon.
Clipping of good afternoon.
One who loots, who steals during a general disturbance such as a riot or natural disaster.
definitions.example: After the hurricane, before law was restored, looters stole everything that wasn't nailed down.

definitions.definition: In a hopeful manner.
It is hoped that; I hope; we hope.
definitions.example: Hopefully, my father will arrive in time for the show.

definitions.definition: The part of the forelimb below the forearm or wrist in a human, and the corresponding part in many other animals.
definitions.example: Her hands are really strong.

definitions.definition: That which resembles, or to some extent performs the office of, a human hand.
In linear measurement:
A side; part, camp; direction, either right or left.
Power of performance; means of execution; ability; skill; dexterity.
An agent; a servant, or manual laborer, especially in compounds; a workman, trained or competent for special service or duty; a performer more or less skillful.
definitions.example: an old hand at speaking;  large farms need many farm hands

definitions.definition: An instance of helping.
definitions.example: Bob gave Alice a hand to move the furniture.

definitions.definition: Handwriting; style of penmanship.
definitions.example: a good hand

definitions.definition: A person's autograph or signature.
definitions.example: Given under my Hand and Seal of the State this 1st Day of January, 2010.

definitions.definition: Personal possession; ownership.
(usually in the plural, hands) Management, domain, control.
definitions.example: in safe hands;  in good hands;  He lost his job when the factory changed hands.  With the business back in the founder's hands, there is new hope for the company.  With John in charge of the project, it's in good hands.

definitions.definition: That which is, or may be, held in a hand at once.
Applause.
definitions.example: Give him a hand.

definitions.definition: A Native American gambling game, involving guessing the whereabouts of bits of ivory or similar, which are passed rapidly from hand to hand.
The small part of a gunstock near the lock, which is grasped by the hand in taking aim.
A whole rhizome of ginger.
The feel of a fabric; the impression or quality of the fabric as judged qualitatively by the sense of touch.
definitions.example: This fabric has a smooth, soft hand.

definitions.definition: Actual performance; deed; act; workmanship; agency; hence, manner of performance.
Agency in transmission from one person to another.
definitions.example: to buy at first hand (from the producer, or when new);  to buy at second hand (when no longer in the producer’s hand, or when not new);  It's not a rumor. I heard it at first hand.

definitions.definition: Rate; price.
To give, pass, or transmit with the hand, literally or figuratively.
definitions.example: He handed them the letter.   She handed responsibility over to her deputy.

definitions.definition: To lead, guide, or assist with the hand; to conduct.
definitions.example: to hand a lady into a carriage

definitions.definition: To manage.
To seize; to lay hands on.
To pledge by the hand; to handfast.
(said of a sail) To furl.
To cooperate.
The process by which something is dried.
definitions.example: This towel is still damp: I think it needs another dry.

definitions.definition: A prohibitionist (of alcoholic beverages).
(with "the") The dry season.
An area of waterless country.
(UK politics) A radical or hard-line Conservative; especially, one who supported the policies of British Prime Minister Margaret Thatcher in the 1980s.
To lose moisture.
definitions.example: The clothes dried on the line.

definitions.definition: To remove moisture from.
definitions.example: Devin dried her eyes with a handkerchief.

definitions.definition: To be thirsty.
To exhaust; to cause to run dry.
For an actor to forget his or her lines while performing.
Free from or lacking moisture.
definitions.example: This towel's dry. Could you wet it and cover the chicken so it doesn't go dry as it cooks?

definitions.definition: Unable to produce a liquid, as water, oil, or (farming) milk.
definitions.example: This well is as dry as that cow.

definitions.definition: Built without or lacking mortar.
Anhydrous: free from or lacking water in any state, regardless of the presence of other liquids.
definitions.example: Dry alcohol is 200 proof.

definitions.definition: Athirst, eager.
Free from or lacking alcohol or alcoholic beverages.
definitions.example: Of course it's a dry house. He was an alcoholic but he's been dry for almost a year now.

definitions.definition: Describing an area where sales of alcoholic or strong alcoholic beverages are banned.
definitions.example: You'll have to drive out of this dry county to find any liquor.

definitions.definition: Free from or lacking embellishment or sweetness, particularly:
(somewhat derogatory) Involving computations rather than work with biological or chemical matter.
(of a sound recording) Free from applied audio effects.
Without a usual complement or consummation; impotent.
definitions.example: never dry fire a bow; dry humping her girlfriend; making a dry run

definitions.definition: Of a mass, service, or rite: involving neither consecration nor communion.
A contiguous area of land, smaller than a continent, totally surrounded by water.
An entity surrounded by other entities that are very different from itself.
definitions.example: an island of colors on a butterfly's wing

definitions.definition: A superstructure on an aircraft carrier's deck.
A traffic island.
definitions.example: the island in the middle of a roundabout

definitions.definition: An unincorporated area wholly surrounded by one or more incorporated areas.
(grammar) A phrase from which a wh-word cannot be extracted without yielding invalid grammar.
To surround with water; make into an island.
To set, dot (as if) with islands.
To isolate.
An evergreen tree of the genus Citrus such as Citrus sinensis.
The fruit of an orange tree; a citrus fruit with a slightly sour flavour.
The colour of a ripe fruit of an orange tree, midway between red and yellow.
Orange juice.
Orange coloured and flavoured cordial.
Orange coloured and flavoured soft drink.
To color orange.
To become orange.
Having the colour of the fruit of an orange tree; yellowred; reddish-yellow.
To put to death; to extinguish the life of.
definitions.example: Smoking kills more people each year than alcohol and drugs combined.

definitions.definition: To render inoperative.
definitions.example: He killed the engine and turned off the headlights, but remained in the car, waiting.

definitions.definition: To stop, cease or render void; to terminate.
definitions.example: My computer wouldn't respond until I killed some of the running processes.

definitions.definition: To amaze, exceed, stun or otherwise incapacitate.
definitions.example: That joke always kills me.

definitions.definition: To cause great pain, discomfort or distress to.
definitions.example: These tight shoes are killing my feet.

definitions.definition: To produce feelings of dissatisfaction or revulsion in.
definitions.example: It kills me to learn how many poor people are practically starving in this country while rich moguls spend such outrageous amounts on useless luxuries.

definitions.definition: To use up or to waste.
definitions.example: He told the bartender, pointing at the bottle of scotch he planned to consume, "Leave it, I'm going to kill the bottle."

definitions.definition: To exert an overwhelming effect on.
definitions.example: Between the two of us, we killed the rest of the case of beer.

definitions.definition: To overpower, overwhelm or defeat.
definitions.example: The team had absolutely killed their traditional rivals, and the local sports bars were raucous with celebrations.

definitions.definition: To force a company out of business.
To produce intense pain.
definitions.example: You don't ever want to get rabies. The doctor will have to give you multiple shots and they really kill.

definitions.definition: To punish severely.
definitions.example: My parents are going to kill me!

definitions.definition: To strike (a ball, etc.) with such force and placement as to make a shot that is impossible to defend against, usually winning a point.
To cause (a ball, etc.) to be out of play, resulting in a stoppage of gameplay.
To succeed with an audience, especially in comedy.
To cause to assume the value zero.
(IRC) To disconnect (a user) involuntarily from the network.
To deadmelt.
(of steel) deoxidized
Any organic matter present in a sedimentary rock that is insoluble in organic solvents; the precursor of oil and natural gas
An act of putting arms around someone and bringing the person close to the chest; a hug.
An enclosure partially or fully surrounding someone or something.
Full acceptance (of something).
An act of enfolding or including.
To clasp (someone or each other) in the arms with affection; to take in the arms; to hug.
To seize (something) eagerly or with alacrity; to accept or take up with cordiality; to welcome.
definitions.example: I wholeheartedly embrace the new legislation.

definitions.definition: To submit to; to undergo.
To encircle; to enclose, to encompass.
To enfold, to include (ideas, principles, etc.); to encompass.
definitions.example: Natural philosophy embraces many sciences.

definitions.definition: To fasten on, as armour.
To accept (someone) as a friend; to accept (someone's) help gladly.
To attempt to influence (a court, jury, etc.) corruptly; to practise embracery.
Any radioactive substance.
Exhibiting radioactivity.
Dangerous and disgusting, particularly of people or ideas.
definitions.example: Even sleazy tactics and his radioactive mouth may not be able to contain this debacle.

definitions.definition: A tank ship, a vessel used to transport large quantities of liquid.
A tank truck.
A fuel tanker, petrol tanker, road tanker.
A tank car
Member of a tank crew, or of an armoured unit.
A longboard.
definitions.example: I swung the tanker around just in time to take off with the lip

definitions.definition: To transport (oil, etc.) in a tanker.
A small wheel or roller, specifically the wheel of a gun carriage.
The ball on top of a flagpole.

On a wooden mast, a circular disc (or sometimes a rectangle) of wood near or at the top of the mast, usually with holes or sheaves to reeve signal halyards; also a temporary or emergency place for a lookout. "Main" refers to the mainmast, whereas a truck on another mast may be called (on the mizzenmast, for example) "mizzen-truck".
A semi-tractor ("semi") trailer; a lorry.
definitions.example: Mexican open-bed trucks haul most of the fresh produce that comes into the United States from Mexico.

definitions.definition: Any motor vehicle designed for carrying cargo, including delivery vans, pickups, and other motorized vehicles (including passenger autos) fitted with a bed designed to carry goods.
A garden cart, a two-wheeled wheelbarrow.
A small wagon or cart, of various designs, pushed or pulled by hand or pulled by an animal, as with those in hotels for moving luggage, or in libraries for transporting books.
A pantechnicon (removal van).
A flatbed railway car; a flatcar.

A pivoting frame, one attached to the bottom of the bed of a railway car at each end, that rests on the axle and which swivels to allow the axle (at each end of which is a solid wheel) to turn with curves in the track; a bogie.
The part of a skateboard or roller skate that joins the wheels to the deck, consisting of a hanger, baseplate, kingpin, and bushings, and sometimes mounted with a riser in between.
A platform with wheels or casters.
Dirt or other messiness.
To drive a truck: Generally a truck driver's slang.
To convey by truck.
definitions.example: Last week, Cletus trucked 100 pounds of lumber up to Dubuque.

definitions.definition: To travel or live contentedly.
definitions.example: Keep on trucking!

definitions.definition: To persist, to endure.
definitions.example: Keep on trucking!

definitions.definition: (film production) To move a camera parallel to the movement of the subject.
To fight or otherwise physically engage with.
To run over or through a tackler in American football.
Swollen or inflated in shape, as if filled with air; pillow-like.
Coming or exhaling in puffs.
definitions.example: A puffy fat man in a tracksuit came jogging around the corner.

definitions.definition: Speaking or writing in an exaggeratedly eloquent and self-important manner.
A final statement of terms or conditions made by one party to another, especially one that expresses a threat of reprisal or war.
A jugular vein.
(by extension) Any critical vulnerability.
definitions.example: It was vicious; he went for the jugular.

definitions.definition: Relating to, or located near, the neck or throat.
(of fish) Having ventral fins attached under the throat.
Relating to juggling.
A compulsive or irrational preoccupation.
An unhealthy fixation.
Influence or control by evil spirits without possession.
Out of order; not working.
definitions.example: Her marriage went kaput.

definitions.definition: A mammal of the order Proboscidea, having a trunk, and two large ivory tusks jutting from the upper jaw.
Anything huge and ponderous.
Used when counting to add length, so that each count takes about one second
definitions.example: Let's play hide and seek. I'll count. One elephant, two elephant, three elephant...

definitions.definition: Ivory.
A large size of paper, measuring 23 by 28 inches, or sometimes 22 by 30, or (double elephant paper) 26 by 40 inches.
A thin, spirally coiling stem that attaches a plant to its support.
A hair-like tentacle.
To congregate in or head towards a place in large numbers.
definitions.example: People flocked to the cinema to see the new film.

definitions.definition: To flock to; to crowd.
To treat a pool with chemicals to remove suspended particles.
To coat a surface with dense fibers or particles.
Having split, bushy ends (of bristles).
A situation which allows a mutually exclusive choice between two or more possibilities; a choice between two or more possibilities.
One of several mutually exclusive things which can be chosen.
The remaining option; something available after other possibilities have been exhausted.
Alternative rock
Relating to a choice between two or more possibilities.
definitions.example: an alternative proposition

definitions.definition: Other; different from something else.
Not traditional, outside the mainstream, underground.
Alternate, reciprocal.
In rhythm, the second half of a divided beat.
As a coordinating conjunction; expressing two elements to be taken together or in addition to each other.
(heading) Expressing a condition.
A contract killer; especially one paid by mafia.
Goalscorer, someone who scores goals.
A readiness in performance; aptness at doing something.
A petty contrivance; a toy.
Something performed, or to be done, requiring aptness and dexterity.
To crack; to make a sharp, abrupt noise; to chink.
To speak affectedly.
A Taser, a handheld device made by Taser International intended to immobilize another by delivering an electric shock.
(by extension) Any electroshock stun gun.
To shock a person, thing, individual, or animal with a handheld device with the electric shock that it delivers; to stun with a stun gun.
To strike verbally or gesturally with ill intents.
definitions.example: As I spoke too loudly during the solemn church service, my husband shot me a glance that emotionally tasered me to the point of silence until we returned home 2 hours later.

definitions.definition: One's structure or make-up: form, shape, bodily proportions.
An important or main item.
A long, prominent article or item in the media, or the department that creates them; frequently used technically to distinguish content from news.
Any of the physical constituents of the face (eyes, nose, etc.).
A beneficial capability of a piece of software.
The cast or structure of anything, or of any part of a thing, as of a landscape, a picture, a treaty, or an essay; any marked peculiarity or characteristic.
definitions.example: one of the features of the landscape

definitions.definition: Something discerned from physical evidence that helps define, identify, characterize, and interpret an archeological site.
Characteristic forms or shapes of parts. For example, a hole, boss, slot, cut, chamfer, or fillet.
(machine learning) An individual measurable property or characteristic of a phenomenon being observed.
The act of being featured in a piece of music.
The elements into which linguistic units can be broken down.
To ascribe the greatest importance to something within a certain context.
To star, to contain.
To appear, to make an appearance.
To have features resembling.
An official document normally used for international journeys, which proves the identity and nationality of the person for whom it was issued.
definitions.example: You will have to bring your passport to prove who you are.

definitions.definition: (by extension) Any document that allows entry or passage.
Something which enables someone to do or achieve something.
definitions.example: The tenor's voice was his passport to the international concert circuit.

definitions.definition: Contrary to logic; lacking sense or sound reasoning.
definitions.example: I received an illogical reply and that left me standing there feeling confused.

definitions.definition: To run a processor (CPU), or any electronic logic device, at a speed higher than is recommended by the manufacturer.
The setting, on a portable electronic device, that causes it to vibrate rather than sound any (or most) needed alarms.
definitions.example: Please put your cellphones on vibrate for the duration of the meeting.

definitions.definition: To shake with small, rapid movements to and fro.
To resonate.
definitions.example: Her mind was vibrating with excitement.

definitions.definition: To brandish; to swing to and fro.
definitions.example: to vibrate a sword or a staff

definitions.definition: To mark or measure by moving to and fro.
definitions.example: a pendulum vibrating seconds

definitions.definition: To affect with vibratory motion; to set in vibration.
To please or impress someone.
To use vibrato.
A smaller, self-contained part of a larger entity. Often refers to a manufactured object that is part of a larger device.
definitions.example: A CPU is a component of a computer.

definitions.definition: Making up a larger whole; as a component word.
Made up of smaller complete units in combination; as a component stereo.
A (usually self-sustaining) chemical reaction involving the bonding of oxygen with carbon or other fuel, with the production of heat and the presence of flame or smouldering.
An instance of this chemical reaction, especially when intentionally created and maintained in a specific location to a useful end (such as a campfire or a hearth fire).
definitions.example: We sat about the fire singing songs and telling tales.

definitions.definition: The occurrence, often accidental, of fire in a certain place, causing damage and danger.
definitions.example: During hot and dry summers many fires in forests are caused by regardlessly discarded cigarette butts.

definitions.definition: The aforementioned chemical reaction of burning, considered one of the Classical elements or basic elements of alchemy.
A heater or stove used in place of a real fire (such as an electric fire).
The elements necessary to start a fire.
definitions.example: The fire was laid and needed to be lit.

definitions.definition: The bullets or other projectiles fired from a gun.
definitions.example: The fire from the enemy guns kept us from attacking.

definitions.definition: Strength of passion, whether love or hate.
Liveliness of imagination or fancy; intellectual and moral enthusiasm.
Splendour; brilliancy; lustre; hence, a star.
A severe trial; anything inflaming or provoking.
Red coloration in a piece of opal.
To produce one or more copies of a text or image on a surface, especially by machine; often used with out or off: print out, print off.
definitions.example: Print the draft double-spaced so we can mark changes between the lines.

definitions.definition: To produce a microchip (an integrated circuit) in a process resembling the printing of an image.
definitions.example: The circuitry is printed onto the semiconductor surface.

definitions.definition: To write very clearly, especially, to write without connecting the letters as in cursive.
definitions.example: I'm only in grade 2, so I only know how to print.

definitions.definition: To publish in a book, newspaper, etc.

definitions.definition: To stamp or impress (something) with coloured figures or patterns.
definitions.example: to print calico

definitions.definition: To fix or impress, as a stamp, mark, character, idea, etc., into or upon something.
To stamp something in or upon; to make an impression or mark upon by pressure, or as by pressure.
To display a string on the terminal.
To produce an observable value.
definitions.example: On March 16, 2020, the S&P printed at 2,386.13, one of the worst drops in history.

definitions.definition: The process or business of producing printed material by means of inked type and a printing press or similar technology.
Material that has been printed.
All the copies of a publication that have been printed in one batch.
Written characters that are not joined up.
To roll (something) down; to unroll.
To be inherited by someone else; to pass down upon the next person in a succession, especially through failure or loss of an earlier holder.
To delegate (a responsibility, duty, etc.) on or upon someone.
To fall as a duty or responsibility on or upon someone.
To degenerate; to break down.

definitions.definition: The commodities sold by a grocer or in a grocery store.

definitions.definition: (usually groceries) retail foodstuffs and other household supplies.
A shop or store that sells groceries; a grocery store.
To go grocery shopping.
To furnish with groceries.
To live or reside in.

definitions.definition: To be present in; to occupy.

definitions.definition: Having relevant and crucial value.

definitions.definition: Pompous; self-important.
(game of go) A local shape to which the ko rule applies; a ko shape.

definitions.definition: (game of go) ko fight
definitions.example: Black wins the ko easily.

definitions.definition: (game of go) a stone in a ko in atari, a ko stone

definitions.definition: The act of thwacking; a strike or blow, especially with a flat implement.
A heavy slapping sound.
To hit with a flat implement.
To beat.
To fill to overflow.

Any of various parrots of the genera Ara, Anodorhynchus, Cyanopsitta, Orthopsittaca, Primolius and Diopsittaca of Central and South America, including the largest parrots and characterized by long sabre-shaped tails, curved powerful bills, and usually brilliant plumage.
A cheerful attitude; happiness; a good, happy, or positive mood.
That which promotes good spirits or cheerfulness; provisions prepared for a feast; entertainment.

One's facial expression or countenance.
One's attitude, mood.
To gladden; to make cheerful; often with up.

definitions.definition: To infuse life, courage, animation, or hope, into; to inspirit; to solace or comfort.

definitions.definition: A small fruit, usually red, black or yellow, with a smooth hard seed and a short hard stem.
Prunus subg. Cerasus, trees or shrubs that bear cherries.

(in the plural) Tea leaves.

The layer of fat supporting the kidneys of a pig, leaf fat.
One of the teeth of a pinion, especially when small.
Marijuana.

To have a consequence or remnant.
To depart; to separate from.
To transfer something.
To remain (behind); to stay.
To stop, desist from; to "leave off" (+ noun / gerund).
The action of the batsman not attempting to play at the ball.

definitions.definition: To give leave to; allow; permit; let; grant.

definitions.definition: To produce leaves or foliage.
To raise; to levy.
Any of various Old-World rodent species belonging to the subfamily Cricetinae.

To snarl or growl; to gnar.
Knotty and misshapen.
Made rough by age or hard work.

Characterized by felicity.
Of a sentence or utterance: semantically and pragmatically coherent; fitting in the context.

definitions.definition: Of considerable or relatively great size or extent.

definitions.definition: Abundant; ample.
Full in statement; diffuse; profuse.
Free; unencumbered.
Unrestrained by decorum; said of language.
Crossing the line of a ship's course in a favorable direction; said of the wind when it is abeam, or between the beam and the quarter.
Any number, proper or improper fraction, or incommensurable ratio.
Of or relating to numbers, especially the characters 0 to 9.

The same in number; hence, identically the same; identical.

Of hail, to fall from the sky.

definitions.definition: To pour down in rapid succession.
Exhibiting a milky iridescence like that of an opal.
A hit or strike with the leg, foot or knee.

definitions.definition: The action of swinging a foot or leg.

definitions.definition: Something that tickles the fancy; something fun or amusing.

definitions.definition: The removal of a person from an online activity.
Any bucking motion of an object that lacks legs or feet.

Pocket.
An increase in speed in the final part of a running race.
To strike or hit with the foot or other extremity of the leg.

definitions.definition: To direct to a particular place by a blow with the foot or leg.

To forcibly remove a participant from an online activity.

In a fond manner; affectionately; tenderly.
Foolishly.

A product of equal factors (and generalizations of this notion): x^n, read as "x to the power of n" or the like, is called a power and denotes the product x \times x \times \cdots \times x, where x appears n times in the product; x is called the base and n the exponent.

An artwork made from marble.
definitions.example: The Elgin Marbles were originally part of the temple of the Parthenon.

definitions.definition: To cause (something to have) the streaked or swirled appearance of certain types of marble, for example by mixing viscous ingredients incompletely, or by applying paint or other colorants unevenly.
To get or have the streaked or swirled appearance of certain types of marble, for example due to the incomplete mixing of viscous ingredients, or the uneven application of paint or other colorants.
To cause meat, usually beef, pork, or lamb, to be interlaced with fat so that its appearance resembles that of marble.
(of meat, especially beef) To become interlaced with fat; (of fat) to interlace through meat.
(by extension) To lace or be laced throughout.
Any of several children's games played with small balls (made of marble or more commonly glass).
definitions.example: Egyptian boys played marbles before the days of Moses.

definitions.definition: (usually in a limited number of expressions) Sanity.
definitions.example: Grandpa's lost his marbles, but at least he still recognizes us.

definitions.definition: Bits of rolled-up rubber shed by the tires of race cars that accumulate at the edges of the track, especially at the corners.
An edible fish of the family Scombridae, often speckled.
